steps.title: Find the main verb
steps.content:
The sentence is: "A plano-convex lens ___ the laser on the center of the 10-millimeter-long crystal ensured a spot size... of 85 micrometers." The main verb is ensured (subject: lens).

So the blank can't be another finite verb — it must be a participial modifier describing the lens.

steps.title: Apply the rule
steps.content:
A modifier describing the lens needs the participle focusing ("a lens focusing the laser..."). "focuses" and "focus" are finite verbs that would create two main verbs (ungrammatical), and "focued" is a misspelling.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
